title: Open well-known paths for custom domain DNS verification

requirements.summary: Configure React app to serve `.well-known` directory as static files without authentication or route interference
acceptanceCriteria: A GET request to `/.well-known/ic-domains` returns the raw text content of the file without redirecting to a login page or rendering any React component.
The `.well-known` directory is served as purely static content from `frontend/public/.well-known/`.
No authentication check or layout wrapping is applied to `/.well-known/*` paths.
The existing `frontend/public/.well-known/ic-domains` file content (canister principal ID) is preserved and served correctly.

file_operations.description: Configure Vite to ensure that requests to `/.well-known/*` paths are served directly from `frontend/public/.well-known/` as static files without being intercepted by the development server or build process. Verify that the `publicDir` is set to 'public' and that static file serving is not blocked by middleware.

Ensure TanStack Router does not intercept `/.well-known/*` paths. Review the router configuration to confirm no catch-all or wildcard routes conflict with static file serving for `.well-known`. If necessary, document that these paths should bypass React routing entirely and be handled by the static file server.

requirements.summary: Ensure TanStack Router does not define routes that intercept `.well-known` paths before static file serving
acceptanceCriteria: The TanStack Router does not render any component in response to `/.well-known/*` URL paths.
Static file serving for `/.well-known/ic-domains` is not blocked by any router-level redirect or 404 handler.

file_operations.description: Review all route definitions in the TanStack Router configuration. Confirm that no route pattern (including catch-all routes like `*` or NotFound handlers) would match `/.well-known/*` paths. If a catch-all or 404 route exists, ensure it explicitly excludes `.well-known` paths or is configured to allow static file serving to take precedence. Document any router-level exclusions or passthrough logic needed for `.well-known` paths.
